MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2010 Off Calendar

JUDICIAL MEDIATION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2010 Continued To Dec-06-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR; IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Christopher J. Keane, Esq. of The Keane Law Firm, P.C. 742-5412 for pltfs & opposing parties; Kathleen L. Morgan, Esq. 994-1342 for defts Seema & Shaheen Hossain; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTIONS ARE DENIED. AS TO ISSUE 1 (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE) A STATUTORY DUTY IS PROPERLY PLED UNDER HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 1596.608, 1596.605 AND 1596.62. AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION) ISSUE 2 GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 818.8 IMMUNITY IS NOT APPLICABLE TO NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS INVOLVING THE RISK OF PHYSICAL HARM. GARCIA V. SUPERIOR COURT (1990) 50 CAL.3D 728. ISSUE 3: SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE OF MISREPRESENTATION EXISTS THAT HOSSAIN'S TRUSTLINE REGISTRY STATUS WAS CURRENT WHEN IN FACT, IT SHOULD HAVE BEEN FORFEITED. THE ACTIONABLE MISREPRESENTATION WAS MADE INDIRECTLY. SEE VARWIG V. ANDERSON (1977) 74 CAL.APP.3D 578. AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF MANDATORY DUTY) ISSUE 4, A MANDATORY DUTY EXISTED TO AUTOMATICALLY FORFEIT TRUSTLINE REGISTRATION AND CONTINUALLY UPDATE THE REGISTRY. HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTIONS 1596.608, 1596.605 AND 1596.62. AS TO ISSUE 5, TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS WERE THE PROXIMATE CAUSE OF INJURY, SINCE HOSSAIN WAS IN FACT REGISTERED AND VERIFICATION BY NELSON WOULD NOT HAVE PREVENTED HER FROM PLACING THE CHILD IN HOSSAIN'S CARE. AS TO THE 6TH ISSUE, A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED AS TO EXACTLY HOW AND WHEN THE CHILD WAS INJURED. THE COURT DECLINES TO RULE ON ISSUES 7 AND 8 AS THEY DO NOT DISPOSE OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437c(f)(1). In addition, the Court indicated it overruled all objections to evidence raised by both parties.(PREV.PRTY PREP.ORD.) (302/CWW)JUDGE:CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE,CSR #5797

Re-Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA Re-Notice of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint. Appearances: Christopher J. Lane, Esq. of the Keane Law Firm, appearing for Plaintiff. There are no other appearances. (This case was called again at 10:22 am.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for false advertising. Remainder of demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff's counsel will prepare Order for the Court's signature. (302/CWW/VC)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
